perturbation.original_text: “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution
perturbation.changed_text: “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Financial Institution rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Banking Organization
perturbation.explanation: Here, we switch Banking Organization with Financial Institution and vice versa. Originally, the definition referred to customers of the broader 'Banking Organization'. Now, it refers to customers of the redefined 'Financial Institution'. Also, the timeframe is prior to termination of employment with a Banking Organization. This is problematic because the core meaning of 'Customer' is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The change swaps 'Banking Organization' and 'Financial Institution' in the definition of 'Customers'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on a clear distinction between these two entities and their respective customers. The non-solicitation clause prohibits soliciting customers of a 'Financial Institution', but the definition now ambiguously defines 'Customers' in relation to termination of employment with a 'Banking Organization'. This creates uncertainty about which customers are protected by the non-solicitation clause, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and impacting enforceability. The definition of customer is altered such that it is inconsistent with the intention of the non-solicitation clause.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees that a Financial Institution, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Banking Organization" to "Financial Institution," we limit the protection only to First Financial and/or HopFed, not the Bank or Heritage. This contradicts the overall intention of the agreement to protect all entities, as initially defined. This limits the scope of available relief, as only specific institutions, based on the latest definition, can claim irreparable damage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Assignments; Successors and Assigns . The rights and obligations of Employee hereunder are not assignable or delegable by Employee, and any prohibited assignment or delegation will be null and void. The Banking Organization may, without the consent of the Employee, assign this Agreement to any successor or in connection with any merger, consolidation, share exchange, combination, sale of stock or assets or similar transaction. The provisions hereof shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the successors and assigns of a Banking Organization.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Banking Organization" to "Financial Institution" narrows the scope of protection. The contradicted text in Section 11 refers to "The Banking Organization" and its rights to assign the agreement. By redefining "Banking Organization" as only "First Financial and/or HopFed", the modification contradicts Section 11, as it now raises questions about whether the assignment rights extend to the initially intended broader group, creating a contradiction and potential ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the assignment clause.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6 , are reasonable and necessary to protect a Banking Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6 , are reasonable and necessary to protect a Financial Institution.
perturbation.explanation: Here, we changed Banking Organization to Financial Institution. This makes the covenants only designed to protect the limited Financial Institution, making the covenants unreasonable as applied to the Bank and Heritage since they are not explicitly included. Thus, a court may find that the restrictions are overly broad and therefore unenforceable as to the Bank and Heritage. Also, this creates contradiction with section 5 and 6, that defines the customers and employees of Banking Organization and the Financial Institutions
perturbation.contradicted_location: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "Banking Organization," which is then changed to "Financial Institution." The explanation states this change makes the covenants only designed to protect the limited Financial Institution, making the covenants unreasonable as applied to the Bank and Heritage since they are not explicitly included, and thus, a court may find that the restrictions are overly broad and therefore unenforceable as to the Bank and Heritage, contradicting section 5 and 6. The contradicted text refers to both "Banking Organization" and "Financial Institution", applying obligations related to customer and employee solicitation based on an employee's position with a Banking Organization and considering the harm that a Financial Institution would suffer. The change introduces misaligned terminology by changing "Banking Organization" to "Financial Institution", leading to conflicting obligations depending on which term is used. This misalignment creates an in-text contradiction as the contract uses these terms without clearly defining if they are interchangeable, potentially leading to conflicting interpretations and uncertainties in enforcement, particularly concerning the scope of protection and non-solicitation obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of Employee’s employment, Employee shall immediately return to the Bank (if subsequent to the Effective Time) or Heritage (if prior to the Effective Time), all Banking Organizations documents and property, including, but not limited to, Confidential Information, computer discs, manuals, reports, files, memoranda, records, door and file keys, passwords and access codes, and any other physical or tangible things that Employee received, prepared, or helped prepare in connection with the Employee’s employment, and Employee shall not retain any copies, duplicates, reproductions or excerpts thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of Employee’s employment, Employee shall immediately return to First Financial (if subsequent to the Effective Time) or HopFed (if prior to the Effective Time), all Banking Organizations documents and property, including, but not limited to, Confidential Information, computer discs, manuals, reports, files, memoranda, records, door and file keys, passwords and access codes, and any other physical or tangible things that Employee received, prepared, or helped prepare in connection with the Employee’s employment, and Employee shall not retain any copies, duplicates, reproductions or excerpts thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Now, Employee needs to return the documents to First Financial or HopFed, even if he has worked for the Bank or Heritage after the effective time. This contradicts the reasonable business expectation. Furthermore, by explicitly stating return to First Financial or HopFed, any reference to Bank or Heritage becomes unenforceable. In section 5 and 6 where there are the financial banking services, is where the text becomes contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the entity to which the employee must return documents upon termination from 'the Bank (if subsequent to the Effective Time) or Heritage (if prior to the Effective Time)' to 'First Financial (if subsequent to the Effective Time) or HopFed (if prior to the Effective Time)'. The contradiction arises because the contradicted text refers to 'Banking Organization' in the context of non-solicitation of customers, implying a relationship to a specific organization. Changing the return destination to First Financial or HopFed creates a misalignment between the company the employee must return property to and the Banking Organizations mentioned in the non-solicitation clause. This change creates a strong contradiction because the original text implies the employee worked for a 'Banking Organization' (Bank or Heritage) whereas the changed text implies it is 'First Financial or HopFed'. This contradicts the idea that 'Banking Organization' in section 5 and 6 refers to 'Bank or Heritage' from the original text.
